label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 2

extraction.one_line_summary: KRON Andy escaped from the breakaway after neutralization discussions, won from reduced group as GC favorites called truce at 9km-to-go provisional finish
tactical_patterns: GC teams negotiated race neutralization mid-stage after Roglic crash, revealing collective power to override organizers' provisional finish protocol.
Piccolo fought back to breakaway after crashing, demonstrating that provisional finish situations incentivize crash recovery over normal race logic.
Kron attacked fellow breakaway survivors after neutralization point passed, exploiting confusion when peloton momentum stops but breakaway racing continues.

rider_signals.MILESI Lorenzo: negative: abandoned while wearing red jersey at 18km to go, suggesting extreme vulnerability to wet conditions
rider_signals.KRON Andy: positive: attacked breakaway companions after provisional finish, showing opportunism and superior form to exploit quasi-neutralized race situation
rider_signals.PICCOLO Andrea: positive: recovered from crash to rejoin breakaway under neutralization pressure, calculated jersey math while others conserved energy
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: crash triggered team's negotiation for race hold-up, demonstrating Jumbo-Visma's political leverage within peloton
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: crashed at 18km remaining, confirming vulnerability in technical wet conditions despite no further detail
rider_signals.SOBRERO Matteo: positive: secured KOM points despite mechanical, showing breakaway awareness to collect secondary classifications during neutralized stage
